project.title: Site-specific N-glycoproteomic analysis of Intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma and Hepatocellular carcinoma
project.projectDescription: In this study,we performed integrated glycoproteomic and global proteomic analysis of tumor tissues of ICC and HCC to investigate the differences in site-specific glycosylation and proteins between ICC and HCC tumors. The paired paracancer tissue were also included as controls to determine the site-specific glycosylation changes in ICC and HCC tumors. The alteration extents in glycopeptide, global proteome, and normalized glycosylation in different sample groups were systematically compared.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: Tissue samples were prospectively collected from patients undergoing intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma (ICC) or hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC) resection at the First Affiliated Hospital of Xi’an Jiaotong University, China. Proteins were extracted and digested with sequencing grade trypsin. Each peptide and glycopeptide samples underwent triplicate LC-MS/MS runs on an Oritrap Fusion Lumos Mass Spectrometer equipped with an Easy-nLC™ 1200 system.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: The LC-MS/MS date of intact glycopeptides and peptides were searched against an in house software and Proteome Discoverer for site-specific glycosylatio and proteomic analysis, respectively.

references.referenceLine: Li J, Zhao T, Li J, Shen J, Jia L, Zhu B, Dang L, Ma C, Liu D, Mu F, Hu L, Sun S. Precision N-glycoproteomics reveals elevated LacdiNAc as a novel signature of intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma. Mol Oncol. 2022 16(11):2135-2152

identifiedPTMStrings.name: No PTMs are included in the dataset

Characteristics[MaterialType]: Liver tissues (~ 52 mg tissue per sample)
Characteristics[CleavageAgent]: sequencing grade trypsin (Promega, Madison, WI, USA; protein: enzyme, 100 : 1)
Characteristics[NumberOfTechnicalReplica: Each sample underwent triplicate LC - MS/MS runs
Characteristics[PooledSample]: were pooled into one sample prior to TMT labeling.
Characteristics[NumberOfSamples]: In total, 12 tumor samples were collected from patients with ICC (n = 6) and HCC (n = 6), and their paired paracancer tissues (n = 12) were also collected as control samples.
Comment[Instrument]: Orbitrap Fusion Lumos Mass Spectrometer equipped with an Easy - nLC™ 1200 system (Thermo Fisher Scientific, San Jose, CA, USA)
Comment[Separation]: Acclaim PepMap100 C18 column (50 cm, 75 µm i.d., 3 µm)
Comment[GradientTime]: complete gradient profile of 120 min for glycoproteomics
Comment[AcquisitionMethod]: followed by data - dependent HCD MS/MS (resolution 50K, collision energy 20% and 37%) of the 20 most abundant ions using an isolation width of 1.6 Da.
Comment[NumberOfMissedCleavages]: up to two missed cleavages were allowed for trypsin digestion
